mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 250, inflation expectations force rapid portfolio rebalancing away from equities, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in total employment and a fall in the unemployment rate signals robust labor market tightening that boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in over 60% of triggered cases or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of sustained monetary tightening, further increases in the federal funds rate trigger valuation compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or flat during tightening phases, or the signal fails to separate bull and bear markets with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and higher corporate revenue growth, generating positive equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window or 5-day returns show no directional bias following production increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 2.0 percent, higher discount rates outweigh earnings growth, forcing a systematic decline in S&P 500 valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: The threshold crossing fails to predict negative returns with a win rate below 40% over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction permits combined with increasing retail sales indicate synchronized consumer and housing demand that supports corporate profit margins and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative coefficient in predictive models or quarterly forward returns are negative in more than 50% of co-rising instances.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, a sudden spike in the unemployment rate signals an impending recession that rapidly erodes equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Unemployment spikes during late cycles fail to predict negative returns, or the signal shows positive predictive power during early-cycle phases instead.
